what_it_does: Renders a fixed 1600x900 presentation-style HTML composition titled "Gen3 — ordinal colour (v2)" with three full-width horizontal bands showing stations, hands, and figures, using color, order, scale, and icons to express ordinal emphasis.
observations: The page is a single static HTML document with embedded CSS and a small script for icon injection.
The viewport is fixed at 1600px by 900px and centered in the browser body.
The layout uses three full-bleed stacked bands rather than floating card rows.
The title area says "One Axis, Read in Order" and "Position Carries the Colour".
The first band shows five pill labels: Quarry, Atelier, Almanac, Harbour, Kiln.
The second band shows six circular hand markers labeled C, D, K, B, S, and F with small role captions.
The third band shows numeric figures including 365, 9, 1:1, 12h, 3, and 1,280°.
Icons are populated at runtime through DNA.org.icon from /surface/runtime/organisms.js.
Styling depends heavily on custom CSS variables from /surface/styles/vars.css.
A faint blueprint grid, bottom hatch wash, and circular V mark add framing detail.
standouts: Three full-width horizontal bands form the organizing structure.
Ordinal emphasis is expressed through sequence, color intensity, border tone, and numeric scale.
The page explicitly labels its layout rule with the comment "zones, not floating rows".
conventions: Uses embedded page-local CSS for the whole composition.
Uses CSS custom properties for spacing, type, color, and surface tokens.
Uses absolute positioning for the outer composition and title/footer details.
Uses flexbox to distribute band contents horizontally.
Injects shared icon markup through a runtime organism helper rather than hardcoding SVG in the document.
concerns: The composition is fixed to 1600x900 and does not include responsive behavior.
The page depends on DNA.org.icon existing at runtime; there is no local fallback if the script fails.
Several visual details depend on external CSS variables, so the file is not self-contained.
Icons are inserted with innerHTML, which assumes trusted output from the icon helper.
notes: A static ordinal-color composition using three labeled bands to show ordered stations, roles, and figures.
questions: Are the station, hand, and figure labels symbolic placeholders or tied to a specific domain vocabulary?
Is the fixed 1600x900 canvas intentional for screenshot/export use?
What does the circular "V" mark denote in this composition?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it presents one ordered axis where position, color, and emphasis communicate ordinal structure.
intention_signals: The file seems to be trying to demonstrate how ordinal position can carry visual emphasis without turning each item into an isolated card or ranked list.
novelty: The composition treats horizontal bands as continuous zones while using pills, circular initials, and baseline-aligned figures as three different representations of ordered material.
contribution: This file contributes a zone-based ordinal layout pattern: full-width horizontal bands, tokenized color progression, runtime icon augmentation, and mixed item forms for labels, roles, and quantitative figures.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML/CSS presentation canvas for Gen3 ordinal colour, with three full-width bands showing ordered stations, hand roles, and figures using CSS variables and runtime DNA icons.
intention_for_embedding: The file was made to express an ordered axis where position and visual treatment carry color and emphasis across several substrate types.
